description: In a bustling coffee shop, Chandler, Monica, Ross, and Joey sit on and around a large orange sofa near a coffee table.
Monica says, "Do we have to tell her?" Ross replies, "Yes, we have to tell her." Monica says, "But it's made her so happy," pressing her hands together.
Ross leans forward, gesturing with one open hand.
Ross raises a finger and says, "Little girl misses her cat."
Ross opens his hands outward and says, "Crazy lady thinks her mother is in a cat," as audience laughter is heard.
Ross stands up from the sofa.
Standing, Ross says, "Okay, you know what?"
Ross gestures while saying, "I have to go have dinner with my son," overlapping with audience laughter.
Ross leans over the table and says, "Can I trust that when you see Phoebe, you will tell her?" The group answers, "Yes."
Ross nods.
Ross says, "Thank you."
Ross walks away. Rachel crosses her arms and watches him leave.
Rachel says, "I hate it when Ross is right," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler, leaning back on the sofa, says, "He is right, isn't he?"
Chandler gestures with his hands and adds, "You know what? I think this might be one of the times when he's wrong." Joey asks, "You think?"
Chandler drops his hands and says, "Oh, no, he's right."
The setting changes to an outside view of the coffee shop with pedestrians walking past the lit windows, while upbeat instrumental music plays.
Back inside, the group is seated. Phoebe walks up holding a grey cat.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Hi."
The group replies, "Hi," in unison.
Phoebe sits in an armchair next to Joey, keeping the cat in her lap.
Joey points at the cat and says, "Phoebes."
Joey leans his forearms on his knees.
Joey says, "About your mom," and Phoebe says, "yeah," pausing briefly.
Chandler gestures toward Joey. Rachel touches her neck. Joey lightly pets the cat.
Joey asks, "how's that going?"
Phoebe smiles.
Phoebe says, "So great."
Phoebe continues to stroke the cat.
Phoebe says, "We took a nap together today. My mom fell asleep on my tummy and purred." Chandler and Rachel stare at her, and audience laughter plays.
Joey rubs the cat's paw.
Joey says, "That's so sweet," while continuing to touch the cat.
Joey abruptly stands up.
Joey says, "I'm going to get some coffee," and walks away from the seating area as audience laughter is heard.
Monica stands up and walks around the coffee table.
Monica crouches near Phoebe, leaning in.
Monica says, "What'd you say, Joe? I'll be right there." Audience laughter begins.
Monica quickly walks away. Rachel stands up from the sofa.
Rachel says, "Phoebes, I just feel so all right, I'm coming already," and hurriedly walks away, leaving Phoebe alone as audience laughter peaks.
The setting changes to a night view of an apartment building exterior, accompanied by upbeat instrumental music.
Inside an apartment, Rachel sits at a round table looking at a magazine. Monica runs out from a hallway wearing only a towel.
Monica rapidly says, "Not yet, not yet, not yet, not yet, not yet, not yet," running past Rachel toward another room.
Rachel stands up as a knocking sound occurs at the apartment door.
Rachel says, "I'm," adjusting her clothing.
Rachel opens the door to reveal Chip standing in the hallway.
Rachel says, "Hello, Chip." Chip says, "Hey, Rach. How you doing?"
Rachel rests her hands on her hips and says, "I'm great. I'm great. Got a great job at Bloomingdale's. I have wonderful friends."
Rachel continues, "And even though I'm not seeing anyone right now I've really never felt better about myself," as audience laughter plays.
Chip stands with his hands in his jacket pockets and nods.
Chip asks, "So Monica ready?"
Rachel replies, "Hunt."
Rachel steps back into the room.
Rachel says, "She'll see out in a second. So, chip," crossing her arms.
Rachel asks, "How's Amy Welsh?" Chip repeats, "Amy Welsh?" Looking up, he adds, "Wow, I haven't seen her since"
Chip shakes his head slightly. Rachel clasps her hands beneath her chin, as audience laughter is heard.
Chip looks past Rachel and asks, "monica about ready?"
Monica walks out from the hallway wearing a red top.
Monica says, "Hi." Chip says, "Hey. Wow, you look great."
Monica smiles and steps toward the door.
Rachel says, "Well, you guys have fun. Catch you later." Chip points at Rachel and says, "Invite you."
Chip steps out into the hallway. Monica pauses at the doorway and turns to Rachel.
Monica places a hand on Rachel's shoulder and asks, "Are you sure you're okay about this?" Rachel replies, "Actually okay, great," as Monica embraces her.
Monica walks out, and Rachel waves before pulling the door closed.

The setting changes to an empty apartment. Joey stands next to a tall wooden entertainment unit, and Chandler stands nearby with his arms crossed. Joey points at the unit and says, "This is the unit for you, my friend."
Joey gestures with both hands and adds, "Sturdy construction, tons of storage compartments, some big enough to fit a grown man."
Chandler stares at Joey.
Chandler asks, "What?" Joey turns to him and replies, "Oh, yeah, I got in there myself once."
Joey places a hand on his chest.
Joey says, "My roommate bet me $5 that I couldn't. And then he stuck a board through the handles and locked me in," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler continues staring at Joey.
Joey says, "It was funny till I started feeling like I was in a coffin," followed by more audience laughter.
Joey briefly lowers his gaze.
Inside an apartment, Joey and a man in a black cap, identified as the Thief, stand near a large wooden entertainment unit.
The Thief gestures toward the unit. "No, you can't fit in that thing," he says.
The Thief gestures again. "That's not deep enough," he adds.
Joey turns his head slightly toward the unit.
Joey says, "Oh, yeah?"
Joey grabs the handles of the unit doors and pulls them open, then picks up a black boombox from an adjacent shelf.
Holding the boombox, Joey turns back to the Thief and says, "If I can't, I'll knock $5 off the price of the unit."
The Thief uncrosses his arms and extends one hand forward, saying, "All right, you have yourself a deal." Joey nods and replies, "Okay."
Joey places the boombox onto a shelf inside the unit, then steps inside and pulls the doors shut from the inside.
Joey's voice from inside the unit says, "See? I told you."
The Thief picks up a hockey stick leaning against the unit and slides it horizontally through the two door handles, securing the doors. Audience laughter is audible.
"Sometimes I come in here just to get away from it," Joey says from inside the unit.
The Thief walks toward the apartment door, picking up a black bag from the counter as he passes.
Joey says, "Hey, a nickel" from inside the unit.
The Thief exits the apartment, carrying the bag. The scene changes to a city skyline at night, then to an outdoor street where Monica and Chip approach a parked motorcycle.
Chip says, "we are." Monica stops, gesturing toward the motorcycle with open hands, and says, "Oh, my God. You still have the chipper."
Chip turns his head toward Monica and says, "What?" Monica smiles and replies, "That's what we used to call your your motorcycle in high school."
Monica gestures with her hands as she speaks. "You know, a motorcycle is a chopper. Your Chip," she says.
Monica lowers her hands.
"Never mind," Monica adds.
Chip looks at Monica.
Chip smiles and says, "I think it's cute."
Chip places a hand on Monica's shoulder and leans in, kissing her on the lips. She places her hands near his waist.
They separate, and Monica smiles, saying, "Wow."
Monica keeps her gaze on Chip.
"Lipper from the chipper," Monica says.
Chip smiles, laughs briefly, and then turns, bending down toward the motorcycle.
Chip looks up at Monica and asks, "So you still in touch with anyone from high school?"
Monica crosses her arms and says, "There's Rachel, and"
Monica lowers her gaze briefly.
Looking back up, Monica finishes, "that's it."
"How about you?" Monica asks. Chip stands back up and replies, "Oh, yeah."
"I still hang with Simmons and Zaina. And I see spindler a lot. Levine, Kelly," Chip says, shifting his weight.
Chip nods slightly and continues, "And I run into Goldie from time to time."
Chip lists, "Stick, Browns, Olkoff Maguire, JT Beardsley."
Monica extends a hand and asks, "Is that all?" Chip gestures and replies, "You know, after high school,"
"you just kind of lose touch," Chip concludes.
Monica smiles and nods, keeping her arms crossed.
Chip picks up a black helmet from the motorcycle. "Oh, yeah. I ran into Richard Dorfman," he says. Monica asks, "Oh, how is he?" Chip says, "Not so good. Me and Simmons gave him a wedgie."
Chip holds the helmet, smiling. Monica smiles back.
"Isn't he an architect now?" Monica asks. Chip replies, "Yeah, they still wear underwear," and hands the helmet to Monica.
Chip swings his leg over the motorcycle. The scene changes back to the apartment, where Chandler opens the front door and steps inside.
Chandler stands just inside the doorway, looking around. The apartment is missing its furnishings.
Chandler walks forward slowly, stops, and points one finger toward the wooden unit, which is held shut by the hockey stick.
"Oh, my God," Chandler says, his voice loud.
Chandler lowers his hand, holding both arms out to his sides, and looks around the empty room again.
"What" Chandler yells.
Chandler spins around to face the kitchen area, keeping his arms extended outward.
Chandler walks to the wooden unit, pulling the hockey stick out. "Are you all right?" he asks.
"Yeah," Joey's voice answers from inside the unit.
Chandler pulls the unit doors open. Joey is sitting cross-legged inside.
"What happened?" Chandler asks, leaning forward slightly.
Joey raises both hands defensively.
Joey pushes himself forward and says, "Oh, man, he promised he wouldn't take the chairs."
Joey stands up, stepping out of the unit.
Chandler gestures with his free hand. "What the hell happened? How are you locked in there? Where the hell's all of our stuff?" he asks rapidly.
Joey turns to face Chandler and gestures with his hands.
Joey points back at the unit. "Well, this guy came by to look at the unit and he said he didn't think it was big enough to fit a grown man," he explains.
Chandler extends both hands forward, still holding the stick in one hand.
Chandler asks, "You got involuntarily?" Joey places a hand on his chest and replies, "I was trying to make a sale."
Joey steps forward, extending one arm.
Joey lowers his arm and says, "If I ever run into that guy again, you know what I'm going to do?"
"Bend over" Chandler says.
Chandler leans his upper body back slightly. The scene changes to a restaurant booth with wooden paneled walls.
Monica and Chip are seated across from each other at the table. Chip rests his hands near his face while Monica holds a glass.
Chip lowers his hands and says, "and then zaina. Just let one rip," followed by audience laughter.
Monica gives a tight, close-lipped smile and nods slightly.
Monica and Chip continue sitting across from each other at the small table. Monica wears a red top.
"Look, not that I don't enjoy talking about people we went to high school with, because I do," Monica says, raising her hand.
Chip takes a sip from his bottle.
"but maybe we could talk about something else. Like you. I don't even know where you work," Monica says, gesturing slightly.
"You know where I work," Chip says. "I do?" Monica asks. "The," Chip begins.
"movie theater used to come in all the time," Chip says.
Audience laughter is heard. Monica's facial expression changes, her eyes widening slightly.
"You still work at the Multiplex?" Monica asks. "Well, I could give up that job," Chip replies, leaning forward.
Chip smiles. Audience laughter is heard.
"Free popcorn and candy anytime I want. I give you free posters for your room," Chip says, gesturing with his hands.
"Thanks. I'm set," Monica says, keeping her lips pressed together.
Chip smiles.
"Oh, you know what came in the other night?" Chip asks, pointing a finger.
"Peters told me he just went out with Leslie mask in it. Got to third base," Chip says, lowering his volume.
Chip mimics cheering by raising both fists while smiling broadly.
Monica forces a smile and covers her mouth with her hand. Audience laughter plays.
"Do you still live with your parents?" Monica asks. "Yeah, but I can stay out as late as I want," Chip replies.
Monica looks away, rubbing her neck. Audience laughter is heard.

The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night. Inside the mostly empty apartment, Chandler and Joey sit on the floor while Phoebe stands near a large wooden entertainment unit, holding a cat. Rachel stands nearby.
"You know, with all our stuff gone, the unit doesn't seem that big," Chandler says, looking around.
Joey looks at Chandler, his expression blank.
"Why couldn't he have been a kidnapper?" Chandler asks. Audience laughter plays.
Joey stares at Chandler.
"Wow, they really got you guys. Your TV, the chairs," Rachel says, opening the entertainment unit doors. "Yeah," Chandler replies.
"Your microwave, the stereo," Rachel continues. "Oh, man, he took the five of," Joey says, looking at a deck of cards in his hand.
Joey examines the cards in his hand. Audience laughter plays.
"no, here it is," Joey says, pulling a card from the deck.
Joey falls backward onto the floor, holding up the card. Monica enters through the front door.
"Oh, my God. What happened?" Monica asks, looking around the empty room.
"Oh, Joey was born, and then 28 years later, I was robbed," Chandler replies. Audience laughter plays.
Joey jumps up from the floor. Rachel walks over to Monica near the kitchen counter.
"How was your day?" Rachel asks.
Monica turns to face Rachel.
"You know how I always wanted to go out with Chip Matthews in high school?" Monica asks, gesturing with her hand.
"Well, tonight I actually went out with Chip Matthews in high school," Monica continues, raising both hands.
Rachel looks at Monica, her eyebrows slightly drawn together.
"Honey, I'm sorry," Rachel says. "No, it's okay," Monica replies, shaking her head.
"Not only did I get to go out with Chip Matthews, I got to dump Chip Matthews," Monica says, smiling.
Rachel smiles and gasps audibly.
"That's so great," Rachel says.
Monica and Rachel hug. Ross enters the apartment through the front door.
"Hey, so what did the insurance companies say?" Ross asks as he walks into the room.
"Oh, they said you don't have insurance here, so stop calling us," Chandler replies, sitting on the kitchen counter.
Ross looks at Chandler, then turns his gaze toward Phoebe, who is kneeling on the floor petting the cat.
"You didn't tell her?" Ross asks, looking at the others and pointing at Phoebe.
Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Monica all look away or downward, avoiding Ross's gaze.
"Okay, fine," Ross says, sighing.
Ross walks over to Phoebe.
"Phoebes," Ross says, crouching down. "Yeah. Hi," Phoebe replies.
"Hi," Ross says, his volume soft.
"Listen," Ross says, gesturing slightly.
Ross clasps his hands together.
"this cat belongs to a little girl," Ross says.
"There are flyers all over the place," Ross adds, gesturing.
Rachel walks over, holding a flyer.
"Sorry, sweetie," Rachel says, holding out the flyer to Phoebe.
Phoebe takes the flyer from Rachel.
"Hey, we can take her back with you if you want," Ross says, his voice softer than before.
Phoebe looks at the flyer, then gazes down at the cat in her arms.
"but" Phoebe says, pausing.
Phoebe continues to look at the cat.
"she chose to find me. I have to respect her decision, right?" Phoebe says, looking up at Ross.
Joey steps forward and points a finger.
"That's a good call," Joey says.
Ross stares at Joey and gestures for him to stop.
"No. Hey, enough is enough," Ross says, turning back to Phoebe and raising his hands.
"Look, I am sorry that you feel guilty or whatever about spending time with your new mom," Ross says, gesturing emphatically.
"but this is not your old mom. This is a cat," Ross continues, his volume rising.
"Okay, Julio the cat," Ross adds, looking at the flyer.
Ross looks from the flyer back to Phoebe.
"not mom cat," Ross says, pointing at the flyer.
Phoebe looks up at Ross, holding the cat close.
In the apartment living room, Phoebe places the cat onto the top of a wooden shelving unit. Ross stands near her holding a piece of paper.
Phoebe turns away from the cat and faces Ross.
"Ross, how many parents have you lost?" Phoebe asks.
Ross looks at her, keeping his posture still.
"None," Ross says.
"Okay, then you don't know what it feels like when one of them comes back, do you?" Phoebe says, gesturing slightly with her hands.
Ross stares at her. In the background, Joey, Chandler, Rachel, and Monica stand near the kitchen area, watching the exchange in silence.
"I believe this is my mother," Phoebe says, pressing one hand to her chest.
"Even if I'm wrong, who cares?" Phoebe continues, raising her hands outward.
Phoebe keeps her hands raised as she awaits a response.
"Just be a friend, okay? Be supportive," Phoebe says.
Ross breaks eye contact with her and looks down at the paper in his hand.
"I'm sorry," Ross says softly.
Ross looks back up at Phoebe.
"okay?" Ross adds.
Ross shifts his posture and makes a brief, open-handed gesture.
"I don't know what to say," Ross says.
A wider view shows Phoebe standing with her arms crossed, while the other four friends continue to observe from the background.
"You could say you're sorry to her. Mom," Phoebe says.
Ross looks at Phoebe, then glances down again.
In the background, Rachel watches Ross with her hand resting near her chin.
"I think she would like that," Rachel says from the background.
Ross stands silently, his expression neutral.
Ross turns and walks across the carpeted living room toward the shelving unit.
The cat sits on top of the unit as Ross approaches it.
"Come here, come here, come here," Ross says, holding out his hand and rubbing his fingers together.
The cat lowers its head toward his extended hand.
"Mrs. Buffet," Ross says to the cat.
The other friends watch the interaction intently.
"Sorry about what I said," Ross tells the cat.
Ross lifts both hands slightly as he prepares to continue speaking.
"It was insensitive of me to say that you were just a cat," Ross says, gesturing toward the animal.
Phoebe observes him, her arms remaining crossed.
"when clearly you are also the reincarnated spirit of my friend's mother," Ross says slowly, emphasizing his words with slow hand movements.
Phoebe smiles at him.
"Thank you," Phoebe says, uncrossing her arms.
Phoebe steps forward and reaches for the cat.
"We both forgive you," Phoebe says, holding the cat against her chest.
Ross stands quietly with his hands resting at his sides.
"honey, what are you going to do about the little girl?" Monica asks. "Yeah, okay. Listen," Phoebe replies.
"Mom, I hope you know that you still mean a lot to me, and you're welcome to come back anytime," Phoebe says, looking directly at the cat.
"Phoebes, if she could come back as a couch, we'd really appreciate," Chandler says, standing next to Joey.
Phoebe stares blankly at Chandler without responding.
"come on, mom. I'm going to take you home," Phoebe says, walking away. "I'll go with you," Rachel says. "Me, too," Monica adds, and they follow Phoebe toward the door.
Joey and Chandler watch the women leave the apartment.
"Oh, you know, I got an extra futon," Joey says, turning to Chandler.
"Dude, you don't have to brag. We got nothing here," Chandler replies, gesturing widely at their bare living room.
Joey and Chandler stand in the empty room. On-screen text reads: "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE".
The screen cuts to black. White text reads, "Dedicated to the memory of DOROTHY KAUFFMAN".
The setting changes to the empty apartment. Joey and Chandler sit inside a wooden canoe resting on the carpeted floor.
Joey sits in the front holding a wooden oar horizontally across his lap, with Chandler sitting directly behind him. Production credits appear on screen.
Both men sit in silence, their postures relaxed and their gazes directed forward.
They remain motionless in the canoe as the silence persists and more credits roll.
Neither man speaks or moves, holding their still positions in the empty room.
The silence continues as the final credits are displayed across the bottom of the screen.
"Peaceful out," Chandler says, breaking the silence.
"here," Chandler finishes, and they continue to sit quietly.
Chandler wears a blue long-sleeved shirt, while Joey wears a dark shirt and a backwards baseball cap. Chandler's head is tilted to the left.
Chandler and Joey remain seated and motionless in the canoe. Chandler’s gaze is directed forward and slightly downward. Joey leans back with his hand resting near his chin. The room is silent.

Both men continue to sit still in the canoe. In the background, white kitchen cabinetry and a closed blue door are visible. A flat cardboard box lies on the floor near the side of the boat. The silence persists through the end of the segment.
